result:
Experiment pre-registered in `runs/pending_experiment.json` and validated (figcheck PASS).

**Design summary:** Champion is exp 35 (743.07 m, confusability-weighted sampling). Iter-12 probes established the wall is *ranking*, not decode/rotation/operating-point: the true cell is field top-1 on only ~2% of eval crops despite being top-20 (of 1024) on 33–58%. Exp 34 pre-registered exactly the right response — a hardest-impostor margin hinge — but it died to an ENOSPC crash (leaked scratch from prior failed runs) before a single gradient step, so the hypothesis was never actually tested. Disk is clean now (33G free), so this iteration retests that same margin-hinge mechanism on top of the now-improved confusability-weighted champion: it's a loss-only change to `model/model.py`'s `loss_fn`, adding `0.5 · relu(1.0 + s_neg − s_pos)` where `s_pos` is the max logit within the true cell's 3×3 neighborhood and `s_neg` is the max logit among cells more than 3 cells away. Everything else (forward pass, sampler, schedule, calibration, ONNX contract) is untouched.

The implementation brief gives file-level instructions precise enough to apply in one pass, and the architecture figure reuses exp 35's diagram verbatim except recoloring the now-unchanged sampler annotation back to ink and adding a small red glyph/caption for the new margin term.

tool_input.command: ls model/ 2>/dev/null; echo ---; cat /FROZEN 2>/dev/null; echo ---; sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT id,title FROM experiments ORDER BY id;" | head -40

sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_json FROM experiments WHERE arch_json IS NOT NULL ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1;" > /tmp/last_arch.json
cat /tmp/last_arch.json | python3 -m json.tool | head -100
sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_json FROM experiments WHERE arch_json IS NOT NULL ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1;" > /tmp/last_arch.json; wc -l /tmp/last_arch.json
sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_json FROM experiments WHERE arch_json IS NOT NULL ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1;" > /tmp/last_arch.json
cat runs/_iter2_arch.svg | .venv/bin/python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read()))" > runs/_svg_jsonstr.txt; wc -c runs/_svg_jsonstr.txt
